mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market slack that suppresses wage growth and consumer spending, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and housing starts confirms broad-based aggregate demand growth, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in over 55 percent of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices during post-shock recovery phases indicate capacity utilization improvements that boost corporate earnings and generate positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative 20-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of recovery phase observations.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates falling below 0.25 percent trigger aggressive liquidity injections that compress risk premiums and force rapid positive equity repricing within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain negative or flat in over 70 percent of periods where FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates signals structural labor market tightening that pressures margins and suppresses quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of periods exhibiting PAYEMS growth alongside UNRATE declines.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real personal consumption expenditures directly increase corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month without conditional triggers.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 36-month rolling window or RSAFS growth correlates negatively with 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 0.5 percent month-over-month signals accelerating supply-side momentum that triggers rapid positive equity repricing within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 65 percent of months where INDPRO month-over-month growth surpasses 0.5 percent.
